title: After-Action Report — Depth Chart Rotation and Playing-Time Burial Fix
scope: Depth charts should optimize daily to ensure every active player gets meaningful playing time, good players shouldn't sit indefinitely, and players who are truly buried (good but blocked) should end up somewhere they can contribute.

rationale.item: Initial player (Spencer Badosa) appeared to have no hitting ability; investigation revealed he had real hitting stats but zero game stats due to never appearing in a game
The 8 starters were literally the first 8 position players by DB row ID with no sort, so anyone inserted later started every game on the bench regardless of skill

delegation_events.decision: Depth charts should optimize daily for playing time and buried players should eventually force their way in
delegation_events.quote: The follow-up request was clearer: depth charts should optimize daily to ensure every active player gets meaningful playing time, good players shouldn't sit indefinitely, and players who are truly buried (good but blocked) should end up somewhere they can contribute.

actions.description: Sort fielders by bat_score before slicing into starters and bench
Double the rest-day base rate from 0.06 to 0.12 for increased bench-player rotation
Add near-mandatory rest at 8+ consecutive starts to prevent iron-man crowding
Raise rest cap from 2 to 3 for deep benches (when len(bench_fielders) >= 4)
Add bench-starvation guard: force swap when a bench player absent from position_workload encounters starter with 5+ consecutive starts
Add playing_time_buried motivation to trade engine to identify and move buried players

risk_posture: Deliberately not a hard cap on playing time; rest rates will need empirical validation
No position-aware bench swap; a 3B starting at SS because SS is resting is still possible
Position-aware bench swap is deferred for follow-up

validation_claims.claim: Import check passed: python3 -c 'import o27v2.sim; import o27v2.trades'
Syntax check passed: python3 -m py_compile o27v2/sim.py o27v2/trades.py
Live game regression not run due to no seeded DB in environment

non_actions.reason: No changes to how players are created. All active players already have contact/power/eye ratings; there was never a defensive-only player type without hitting stats.
The habit-cup swap was already working correctly; the only issue was the base ordering.
Not changed. The starvation guard uses this as-is.
These exist and are loaded but drive manager AI substitution during a game, not pre-game lineup construction. That separation is correct.

residual_risks: Empirical playing-time validation needed after full season sim
Position-aware bench swap not implemented (3B at SS mismatch still possible)
Trades completing at reasonable rate is untested (acceptance rates may be too low)
Numbers are approximate pending clean re-measure
open_hooks: Empirical playing-time validation after simming a full season
Verify trades actually complete at acceptable rates
section_headers_original: Why bench players rarely got in
Sort fielders by bat_score before slicing
Raise rest cap from 2 to 3 for deep benches
parser_notes: Clear diagnostic of lineup-selection bug (no sort, insertion-order only). Conservative on changes with clear open items for empirical validation. Good documentation of non-changes explaining why.
